data.title: As a developer, CI skeletons generated by architect-foundation use hashFiles() conditionals with isolated per-stack blocks
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update architect-foundation's CI skeleton generation so that each language-setup step is wrapped in an appropriate hashFiles() conditional (e.g., `if: hashFiles('package-lock.json') != ''` for Node). All hashFiles() paths must be root-relative without leading slashes. Per-stack conditional blocks must be isolated from each other so Epic F can append a Java block without modifying existing blocks.

**Why**: The current CI skeleton uses static language-setup YAML with no hashFiles() conditionals. This means the CI skeleton runs every language setup step on every project, regardless of stack — wasting build minutes and causing unnecessary setup failures.

**Scope**:
- IN: hashFiles() conditional wrapping for existing Python, Node/JS, and Ruby CI skeleton language-setup steps; root-relative paths (e.g., 'requirements.txt' not './requirements.txt'); per-stack blocks isolated (each in its own conditional block, not interleaved); update architect-foundation/SKILL.md with the updated CI skeleton template; validation: parse generated YAML and assert hashFiles() paths are root-relative
- OUT: Java CI skeleton block (Epic F Story F3 or F6 adds the Java block); pre-filling commands.* (Story E1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a CI skeleton generated for a Node/JS project includes a language-setup step conditionally gated on `hashFiles('package-lock.json') != ''` (or `yarn.lock`) using root-relative path
  ← Satisfies: "CI skeleton generation uses hashFiles() conditional pattern; all hashFiles() paths root-relative without leading slashes"
- When this story is complete, a CI skeleton generated for a Python project includes a language-setup step conditionally gated on `hashFiles('requirements.txt') != ''` (or pyproject.toml/poetry.lock) using root-relative path
  ← Satisfies: "each language-setup step wrapped in appropriate lockfile check"
- When this story is complete, each language block in the generated YAML is isolated in its own conditional — a new Java block can be appended after the Ruby block without modifying any existing block's conditional logic
  ← Satisfies: "per-stack conditional blocks are isolated so Epic F can append a Java block without modifying existing blocks"
- When this story is complete, unit tests parse the generated GitHub Actions YAML and assert: hashFiles() conditionals present, paths are root-relative (no leading slash), and blocks are structurally isolated
  ← Satisfies: SC6 validation for CI skeleton

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] This story modifies architect-foundation/SKILL.md — which Story E1 also modifies (different sections). Story E2 must depend on E1 to avoid edit conflicts. Story E2 modifies the CI skeleton section; Story E1 modifies the config-write section.
- [Testing] Parse generated YAML with PyYAML or similar; assert on the 'if' clause values specifically; do not assume the YAML structure is fixed

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
